title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Should the Death Penalty Be Abolished?
prompt_html:
A number of countries continue to use capital punishment for the most serious crimes, while others have removed it from their legal systems entirely.

To what extent do you agree or disagree that the death penalty should be abolished everywhere?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Do Celebrities Deserve Their High Salaries?
prompt_html:
Famous actors, singers and sports stars often earn far more money than professionals such as doctors, teachers and scientists whose work benefits society directly.

To what extent do you agree or disagree that these high salaries are justified?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Should Governments Prioritise Public Transport Over Roads?
prompt_html:
As cities expand, some people believe that governments should spend most of their transport budget on buses, trains and trams rather than on building and widening roads for cars.

To what extent do you agree or disagree with this view?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Should Second Languages Be Taught in Primary School?
prompt_html:
Some educationalists argue that children should begin learning a foreign language in the first years of primary school, while others feel this age should be reserved for mastering their first language.

To what extent do you agree or disagree that a second language should be taught from an early age?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Is Advertising Junk Food to Children Harmful?
prompt_html:
Manufacturers of sugary snacks and fizzy drinks frequently target their advertising at young children through television, apps and social media.

To what extent do you agree or disagree that this kind of advertising should be banned completely?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Homeschooling Versus Traditional Schooling
prompt_html:
A growing number of parents choose to educate their children at home rather than sending them to a conventional school. Supporters praise the flexibility this offers, but critics worry about its effects on children.

Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Nuclear Power - Solution or Threat?
prompt_html:
Some experts see nuclear energy as a clean and reliable way to meet growing electricity demand, while others regard it as dangerous and too costly to justify.

Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Should Online News Be Free or Paid For?
prompt_html:
Many newspapers now charge readers a subscription to access their websites. Some people believe reliable journalism must be paid for, while others argue that access to news should be free for everyone.

Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Genetic Modification of Food Crops
prompt_html:
Scientists are increasingly able to alter the genes of food crops to make them grow faster or resist disease. Some welcome this as a way to feed a growing population, while others fear unknown consequences for health and nature.

Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Traditional Remedies or Modern Medicine?
prompt_html:
In many parts of the world, people still rely on traditional herbal remedies passed down through generations, while others trust only modern, scientifically tested medicine.

Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Youth Unemployment in Modern Economies
prompt_html:
In many countries, large numbers of young people finish their education but are unable to find a job for a long period of time.

What problems does youth unemployment cause, and what measures could be taken to reduce it?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Decline of Bee Populations
prompt_html:
Bees and other pollinating insects, which are essential for growing many of the world's crops, are disappearing at an alarming rate in several regions.

What are the causes of this decline, and what can be done to address it?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Overcrowding at Popular Tourist Destinations
prompt_html:
Some famous cities and natural sites now receive so many visitors that local residents and the environment are put under serious strain.

What problems are caused by this over-tourism, and what solutions would you suggest?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Rise of Household Debt
prompt_html:
In many societies, ordinary families are borrowing more and more money through credit cards and loans, and personal debt has reached record levels.

What problems can high levels of household debt cause, and what steps could be taken to tackle them?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Digital Addiction Among Young People
prompt_html:
Many teenagers find it difficult to put down their phones and spend a large part of each day on games, videos and social media.

What problems does this dependence on digital devices create, and what solutions can you recommend?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Living in a Multigenerational Household
prompt_html:
In some cultures it is common for grandparents, parents and children to all live together under one roof rather than in separate homes.

Do the advantages of living in a multigenerational household outweigh the disadvantages?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Growth of Online Learning Platforms
prompt_html:
It is now possible to take entire university courses online, watching lectures and submitting work over the internet without ever attending a campus.

Do the advantages of studying through online platforms outweigh the disadvantages?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Hosting Major International Sporting Events
prompt_html:
Many cities compete for the right to host large sporting events such as the Olympic Games or the football World Cup, which require enormous investment.

Do the advantages of hosting such events outweigh the disadvantages?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Working for a Large Multinational Company
prompt_html:
Many graduates aim to work for large multinational corporations that operate in dozens of countries rather than for small local firms.

Do the advantages of working for a multinational company outweigh the disadvantages?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Moving Towards a Cashless Society
prompt_html:
In some countries, physical money is being used less and less as people pay for almost everything with cards and phone apps.

Do the advantages of a society without cash outweigh the disadvantages?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Why So Many People Move to Cities
prompt_html:
Across the world, people continue to leave rural areas and move into large cities in ever greater numbers.

Why do so many people choose to move to cities? What effects does this movement have on the countryside they leave behind?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Disappearance of Minority Languages
prompt_html:
Every year, a number of the world's smaller languages stop being spoken as their last remaining speakers pass away.

Why are so many minority languages dying out? Do you think efforts should be made to keep them alive?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Falling Public Trust in the News Media
prompt_html:
Surveys in many countries suggest that people increasingly distrust newspapers, television channels and other traditional sources of news.

Why do you think trust in the news media has fallen? What could be done to rebuild public confidence in journalism?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Why Fewer People Cook Meals at Home
prompt_html:
In many households, home-cooked meals are being replaced by ready-made food and restaurant deliveries.

Why are fewer people preparing meals at home than in the past? What effects does this trend have on families and society?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Growing Popularity of Extreme Sports
prompt_html:
Dangerous activities such as rock climbing, skydiving and white-water rafting are attracting more and more participants around the world.

Why are extreme sports becoming so popular? Do you think governments should discourage people from taking part in them?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Expansion of the Gig Economy
prompt_html:
A rising number of people no longer hold permanent jobs and instead earn a living through short-term tasks arranged by apps and websites, such as delivering food or driving passengers.

Is the growth of this so-called gig economy a positive or negative development?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Spread of the Same Food Brands Worldwide
prompt_html:
The same international restaurant chains and food brands can now be found in almost every country, and people in very different places increasingly eat the same meals.

Is this a positive or negative development?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: The Automation of Factory Work
prompt_html:
Robots and automated machines are carrying out an increasing share of the work in factories that was once done by human hands.

Is this increasing automation of manufacturing a positive or negative development?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: People Marrying and Having Children Later
prompt_html:
In many countries, people are now choosing to marry and to start a family much later in life than previous generations did.

Is this trend towards delaying marriage and children a positive or negative development?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

title: IELTS Writing Task 2: Tourism in Fragile Natural Environments
prompt_html:
Remote and delicate places such as rainforests, coral reefs and polar regions are increasingly being opened up to tourists.

Is the growth of tourism in these fragile environments a positive or negative development?

Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
